name: Doree's Extravagant Almond Cake
recipeInstructions:
This is from Deborah Madison's "The Savory Way" (Bantam, 1990). When the cake cools, slice it horizontally and spread it with raspberry jelly or whole, sugared raspberries. Replace the top and dust with powdered sugar.
1. Preheat the oven to 325°F.
2. Butter and flour a 9-inch springform pan.
3. In a food processor, combine the almond paste and sugar until well blended.
4. Add the butter and continue processing until blended; then add the eggs one at a time, sour cream, and flavorings.
5. Transfer the mixture to a mixing bowl.
6. Sift flour with baking powder, baking soda, and salt.
7. Add the sifted dry ingredients to the batter a third at a time, gently stirring in each cupful.
8. Turn the batter into the prepared pan.
9. Bake until golden on top and springy to the touch, about 1 hour.
10. Remove from oven and let cool slightly; then turn out onto serving plate.
11. Dust with sifted powdered sugar when cool. This cake will keep well, wrapped tightly for several days. Makes 1 large cake.
